summary: Verified header logo visibility regression fix. All 6 requested checks pass: (1) home '/' logo has cinematic brass-cream gradient with drop-shadow — 270x41 px, visible over dark hero; (2) after >40px scroll, logo swaps to logo-emboss (color rgb(20,17,13)) on parchment bar; (3) /portfolio logo is dark embossed and visible against parchment; (4) nav links over hero are white/90 (rgba(255,255,255,0.9)) — legible, active link is brass; (5) Book Consultation btn is brass (rgb(176,141,87)) over hero, dark ink (rgb(20,17,13)) on light pages, links to /contact; (6) navigation Home/Portfolio/Services/Studio(=/about)/Contact works and 4/4 home images load. Zero console errors, zero page errors. Screenshots visually confirm the cinematic brass-cream logotype pops against the dark hero and the embossed dark variant on light pages. Kylie /api/chat 500 remains graceful as expected (LLM budget).
critical_code_review_comments: Navbar.jsx L26: overHero relies solely on `pathname === '/' && !scrolled`. If future non-home routes also have a dark hero (e.g. project detail page hero), the logo will incorrectly render logo-emboss over dark imagery. Consider passing an explicit `variant` prop or reading a context set by the page.

index.css .logo-cinematic uses -webkit-background-clip:text and color:transparent — this correctly hides raw text color, but for browsers without gradient support the logo will be invisible. Add a `@supports not (-webkit-background-clip: text)` fallback to color:#efe3cd for safety.

Navbar Book Consultation button uses `transition-colors` — combined with route navigation, sampling the computed backgroundColor immediately after click reveals mid-transition brass rather than final ink. Not a user-facing bug (final state is correct within ~150ms), but tests that assert style right after click should wait or use expect(...).toHaveCSS with retry.
test_credentials: damien@boyleinteriors.com / Damien2026! (not exercised in this iteration — public UI only)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Header logo regression fix is fully verified. Home hero uses .logo-cinematic (linear-gradient 175deg fbf7ef->efe3cd->d8c4a0->b08d57 clipped to text + drop-shadow); light/scrolled uses .logo-emboss (color #14110d + white-highlight text-shadow). Navbar overHero = pathname==='/' && !scrolled. Nav links testids: nav-link-home/portfolio/services/studio/contact (note: About route uses testid nav-link-studio because the label is 'Studio'). Book btn testid: nav-book-btn. Existing backend regression suite: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py.
rca of the issue: Original bug: logo was `text-[#14110d]` unconditionally, so on the dark hero it rendered dark-ink over dark-ink and became invisible. Fix uses conditional class: logo-cinematic (brass-cream gradient via background-clip:text + drop-shadow triple stack) when overHero, else logo-emboss (dark ink with subtle white text-shadow highlight). Verified via computed style (backgroundImage contains 'linear-gradient', filter contains 'drop-shadow', bounding rect 270x41) and visual screenshots.
